reflection:
The morning checkin on Apr 29 said it all: you slept on your back, "Finally a 7.5h sleep". You hadn't done that in ages. WHOOP captured the same night, 94 percent efficiency, 7.5 hours. That was the quiet London hotel night before the flight.

Then the day did its damage. Heathrow, the lounge meal, the flight, and you landed bloated to 6 and your coccyx and pelvic floor both at 5 by evening. You called it yourself, "this flight today absolutely made me super bloated", "longest farts ever". Real. The body had a long day.

Last meal at 20:30 in Ljubljana with almond butter on top, and you wrote in could-be-better that you should have eaten earlier. Apr 28 was 20:00, last night 20:30. The cascade is starting again. You know this one.

The vitamin C call was good, by the way. Two nights ago you woke at 4am wired, pinned it on the C, dropped it, slept fine. That kind of self-correction is exactly the work.

The worry about sitting-bone pain not lifting is real and I hear it. Months of this. But you slept on your back last night and that is the body being slightly less guarded. Don't dismiss the small wins.

One thing for today: finish dinner by 18:30. You wrote it yourself last night. Make it true.
opener: you slept on your back last night, hasn't happened in ages
focus_for_today: finish dinner by 18:30, you named the late-eating pattern yourself last night
